title: Tech News Article
prompt:
Summarize the following article in 2-3 sentences:

OpenAI has announced GPT-5, its most advanced language model to date. The model demonstrates significant improvements in reasoning, coding, and multimodal understanding compared to its predecessor GPT-4. According to OpenAI, GPT-5 achieves state-of-the-art performance on several academic benchmarks including MMLU, HumanEval, and GSM8K. The model also introduces a new safety training approach called Constitutional AI Alignment, reducing harmful outputs by 78% in internal testing. Developers can access GPT-5 through the OpenAI API starting today, with pricing set at $15 per million input tokens and $60 per million output tokens.
source_text: OpenAI has announced GPT-5, its most advanced language model to date. The model demonstrates significant improvements in reasoning, coding, and multimodal understanding compared to its predecessor GPT-4. According to OpenAI, GPT-5 achieves state-of-the-art performance on several academic benchmarks including MMLU, HumanEval, and GSM8K. The model also introduces a new safety training approach called Constitutional AI Alignment, reducing harmful outputs by 78% in internal testing. Developers can access GPT-5 through the OpenAI API starting today, with pricing set at $15 per million input tokens and $60 per million output tokens.
reference_summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5, a model with improved reasoning, coding, and multimodal capabilities that outperforms GPT-4 on benchmarks. It features a new safety technique reducing harmful outputs by 78% and is available via API at $15/$60 per million tokens.

title: Business Report Summary
prompt:
Summarize the following quarterly earnings report:

Acme Corporation reported Q3 2024 earnings with total revenue of $4.2 billion, representing a 12% year-over-year increase. Net income rose to $680 million, up 18% from the same quarter last year. The company's cloud division was the standout performer, growing 34% to reach $1.1 billion in revenue. However, the hardware segment declined 8% to $900 million amid supply chain disruptions. Operating expenses increased 9% to $3.1 billion primarily due to headcount growth in engineering and sales. The company raised its full-year revenue guidance to $16.5 billion, citing strong demand in enterprise software. CEO Jane Smith attributed the growth to successful product launches and international expansion into Southeast Asian markets.
source_text: Acme Corporation reported Q3 2024 earnings with total revenue of $4.2 billion, representing a 12% year-over-year increase. Net income rose to $680 million, up 18% from the same quarter last year. The company's cloud division was the standout performer, growing 34% to reach $1.1 billion in revenue. However, the hardware segment declined 8% to $900 million amid supply chain disruptions. Operating expenses increased 9% to $3.1 billion primarily due to headcount growth in engineering and sales. The company raised its full-year revenue guidance to $16.5 billion, citing strong demand in enterprise software. CEO Jane Smith attributed the growth to successful product launches and international expansion into Southeast Asian markets.
reference_summary: Acme Corp posted Q3 revenue of $4.2B (+12% YoY) and net income of $680M (+18%). Cloud grew 34% to $1.1B while hardware fell 8%. Full-year guidance raised to $16.5B on strong enterprise demand.

title: Medical Research Abstract
prompt:
Summarize this research abstract in plain language:

Background: Type 2 diabetes mellitus affects over 500 million adults globally and is associated with increased cardiovascular mortality. Current first-line treatments include metformin and lifestyle modification, but long-term glycemic control remains challenging. Methods: We conducted a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial of 1,240 patients with poorly controlled T2DM. Participants received either semaglutide 2.4mg weekly or placebo for 52 weeks in addition to standard care. Primary endpoints included HbA1c reduction and body weight change. Results: Semaglutide reduced HbA1c by 1.8 percentage points compared to 0.4 for placebo (p<0.001). Body weight decreased by 12.4 kg in the treatment group versus 2.1 kg in controls. Cardiovascular events occurred in 3.2% of semaglutide patients versus 5.8% in placebo. Conclusion: Semaglutide significantly improves glycemic control, promotes weight loss, and reduces cardiovascular risk in T2DM patients with inadequate glycemic control.
source_text: Background: Type 2 diabetes mellitus affects over 500 million adults globally and is associated with increased cardiovascular mortality. Current first-line treatments include metformin and lifestyle modification, but long-term glycemic control remains challenging. Methods: We conducted a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial of 1,240 patients with poorly controlled T2DM. Participants received either semaglutide 2.4mg weekly or placebo for 52 weeks in addition to standard care. Primary endpoints included HbA1c reduction and body weight change. Results: Semaglutide reduced HbA1c by 1.8 percentage points compared to 0.4 for placebo (p<0.001). Body weight decreased by 12.4 kg in the treatment group versus 2.1 kg in controls. Cardiovascular events occurred in 3.2% of semaglutide patients versus 5.8% in placebo. Conclusion: Semaglutide significantly improves glycemic control, promotes weight loss, and reduces cardiovascular risk in T2DM patients with inadequate glycemic control.
reference_summary: A 52-week trial of 1,240 T2DM patients found weekly semaglutide 2.4mg reduced HbA1c by 1.8 points and body weight by 12.4kg, and cut cardiovascular events nearly in half compared to placebo.

title: Customer Support Interaction
prompt:
Summarize this customer support conversation:

Agent: Hello, thank you for contacting Acme support. How can I help you today?
Customer: Hi, I ordered a laptop three weeks ago, order number 78432, and it still hasn't arrived. The website shows it was delivered but I never received it.
Agent: I apologize for the inconvenience. Let me look up your order... I can see order 78432 was marked delivered on November 3rd at 2:47 PM. According to our records, it was left at the front door.
Customer: I was home all day and no one knocked. My doorbell camera shows no delivery was made.
Agent: I completely understand your frustration. In this case, I'm going to file a lost package claim with our shipping carrier and also initiate a replacement shipment. You should receive the replacement within 3-5 business days with expedited shipping at no extra charge.
Customer: Thank you. Will I get a confirmation email?
Agent: Yes, you'll receive a confirmation email within 30 minutes with your new tracking number.
source_text:
Agent: Hello, thank you for contacting Acme support. How can I help you today?
Customer: Hi, I ordered a laptop three weeks ago, order number 78432, and it still hasn't arrived. The website shows it was delivered but I never received it.
Agent: I apologize for the inconvenience. Let me look up your order... I can see order 78432 was marked delivered on November 3rd at 2:47 PM. According to our records, it was left at the front door.
Customer: I was home all day and no one knocked. My doorbell camera shows no delivery was made.
Agent: I completely understand your frustration. In this case, I'm going to file a lost package claim with our shipping carrier and also initiate a replacement shipment. You should receive the replacement within 3-5 business days with expedited shipping at no extra charge.
Customer: Thank you. Will I get a confirmation email?
Agent: Yes, you'll receive a confirmation email within 30 minutes with your new tracking number.
reference_summary: Customer reported missing laptop (order 78432) marked as delivered but not received. Agent filed a lost package claim and arranged a free expedited replacement shipment arriving in 3-5 business days with confirmation email.

title: Legal Document Summary
prompt:
Summarize this contract clause in plain language:

Section 12.3 Limitation of Liability. Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, in no event shall either party be liable to the other party for any indirect, incidental, consequential, special, exemplary, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of profits, loss of revenue, loss of data, loss of business opportunity, or cost of substitute goods or services, arising out of or related to this Agreement, whether based on breach of contract, tort (including negligence), strict liability, or any other legal theory, even if such party has been advised of the possibility of such damages. The total cumulative liability of either party arising out of or related to this Agreement shall not exceed the greater of (a) the total fees paid or payable by Customer to Company in the twelve (12) month period immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim, or (b) one hundred thousand dollars ($100,000).
source_text: Section 12.3 Limitation of Liability. Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, in no event shall either party be liable to the other party for any indirect, incidental, consequential, special, exemplary, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of profits, loss of revenue, loss of data, loss of business opportunity, or cost of substitute goods or services, arising out of or related to this Agreement, whether based on breach of contract, tort (including negligence), strict liability, or any other legal theory, even if such party has been advised of the possibility of such damages. The total cumulative liability of either party arising out of or related to this Agreement shall not exceed the greater of (a) the total fees paid or payable by Customer to Company in the twelve (12) month period immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim, or (b) one hundred thousand dollars ($100,000).
reference_summary: Neither party can sue the other for indirect damages like lost profits or data. Total liability is capped at whichever is higher: fees paid in the last 12 months or $100,000.
